releases.name: FlowForge v1.1.0 Release Readiness
releases.targetWindow: Release ceremony after the full behavioral-confidence sweep and re-run gates pass
releases.summary: Tracks readiness for agency/autonomy evidence, media proofs, Windows repair proof, CLI design language, release gates, and collateral. GATING DECISION (operator, 2026-06-10): the release's marketing story is the agent-behavior improvements, so behavioral confidence must be extremely high — the FULL brittle-phrase/behavioral-confidence sweep blocks the ship: code freeze at eae78ee5 was deliberately broken after manual exercise surfaced guard misfires and canned-prose-in-prod; the release now gates on the complete sweep (evidence-delivery projection, retiring the keyword intent classifier, grounding all ranked behavioral phrase heuristics, adversarial cases for every live finding, full gate re-run, honest-soak remediation of every scenario the canned-path removal exposes, and the git-history personal-content scrub), not on a partial fix set. CEREMONY PREFLIGHT (2026-06-10): a fresh full behavioral soak on the release HEAD surfaced one more guard over-fire (B10/R-BPS-9): ExecutionTruthGuard force-failed a continuation/correction turn that truthfully RECALLED a prior in-session inspection ('name one file you observed') into an ErrGuardExhausted panic, because the recall made no fresh in-turn tool call. Fixed by grounding the inspection-evidence obligation in turn provenance (recallsPriorObservationTurn, gated on prior assistant context so first-turn fabrication is still caught). Authoritative pre-ship soak on the fixed tree (git_commit d288abd1, cloud-provider-v4-flash, cloud-only per directive): 37/38, 0 release-gate findings, 0 retry waits; correction_semantics now grade A. The sole miss is geopolitical_sitrep — live Wikipedia Current-Events source returned no-extract that instant — re-verified PASS in isolation (live-source variance, not a regression). REMEDIATION COMPLETE (2026-06-10 late): the 37/38 run's residual misses were NOT model variance — recorded turn_diagnostic events proved a cascade of guard/judge/routing defects against recall/continuation turns (B11-B13): content-less web fetches counted as grounding evidence; task_deferral and non_repetition rejected user-requested restatement (recalled filename, named topic anchor); named-topic anchor matching missed the model's U+2011 hyphen rendering; the blocked-impact contract lacked the positive 'did change' token form; the fuzzy scheduling lean misrouted return-to-topic turns; and the grounding judge's 6s timeout (two recorded 'context deadline exceeded' failures) converted over-fires into panics (default now 15s). All fixed with regression locks R-BPS-10/11/12 on commit 1ea584bc. Authoritative pre-ship soak on that commit: 38/38, all 23 families grade A, 0 quality slices below floor, 0 release-gate findings, 0 retry waits — the first fully clean suite on release code. Determinism evidence: correction_semantics 10/10 consecutive isolated PASS, continuity_after_blocked_execution 8/8, geopolitical_sitrep 3/3. Full gates green on the same commit (the unit-test suite 60 ok/0 fail, race, lint 0 issues, vet, linux+windows cross-compile, live smoke). CEREMONY EXECUTION (2026-06-11): PR #90 (release->develop) was the branch's first Linux/Windows CI exercise and surfaced five platform defects + a stdlib vuln, each diagnosed from recorded job logs and fixed at the proven cause (artifact-evaluator cold-GOCACHE timeout, Windows monotonic-clock deadline race, Windows 8.3 tilde over-match, non-atomic test config writes double-firing the mtime-poll watcher, WAL busy_timeout 5s->30s, go 1.26.3->1.26.4); CI fully green on round 2. The post-fix soak then surfaced one more behavioral gap (B14/R-BPS-13): a web-read final ending by ANNOUNCING retrieval it never performed (promissory tail, incomplete turn) passed all guards; ExecutionTruthGuard now rejects promissory-retrieval tails with retry. Final authoritative pre-ship soak on release HEAD 8761b3b2: 38/38, all 23 families grade A, 0 release-gate findings; geopolitical_sitrep 6/6 isolated post-fix. CEREMONY COMPLETE (2026-06-11): merged via PR #90 (develop, da4c3d43) and PR #91 (main, 593b9672); tag v1.1.0 pushed after main's exact-SHA CI went green. First release-workflow run died on an upstream 429 fetching the Piper asset; re-dispatched against the existing tag per runbook item 7 — all 5 platform builds, publish, and self-evaluation green. Published release verified live: 18 assets incl. SHA256SUMS (checksum spot-verified), binary reports 1.1.0 on go1.26.4, /releases/latest resolves FlowForge v1.1.0 (displacing the foreign Architext release as Latest). Site release-sync dispatch succeeded; the site's Vercel deploy remains broken by an EXPIRED VERCEL_TOKEN (set 2026-02-23; deploys worked 2026-05-19, identical failure recorded 2026-06-07 pre-ceremony — pre-existing). VERCEL_ORG_ID/VERCEL_PROJECT_ID secrets corrected to API ground truth; token rotation is operator-only (Vercel dashboard), after which deploy.yml re-dispatch publishes the synced v1.1.0 site data.

releases.name: v1.1.1 hotfix
releases.targetWindow: ASAP same-day hotfix ceremony per docs/testing/release-procedure.md
releases.summary: Hotfix for two defects proven from recorded state during the v1.1.0 release-day live incident. H1: the daemon ran half-alive after an API bind failure (recorded 'address already in use' ERR at daemon_subsystems.go:94 while channels/agency kept running and clients reached a stale dev server squatting the port) — binding is now synchronous and bind/serve failures stop the daemon. H2: the semantic response cache is removed entirely (operator decision 2026-06-11) — a cached context-bound directive answered a greeting and contaminated session state; production evidence: zero stores since 2026-04-16, tokens_saved=0 lifetime, entirely pathological hit population (stale clock served 11x), and behavioral soaks always wiped it so certified behavior was already cache-off. Removal spans pipeline stage, llm two-tier cache, semantic_cache table (migration 065, verified against a copy of production data), eviction task, cached guard preset, and admin/stats surfaces (wire shapes retained, truthfully zero). In-flight request dedup retained. Completes the advisory-pipeline ADR's cache-removal mandate. Deferred (justified): dashboard cache-panel + wire-key removal is a frontend contract change shipping with the next flowforge-site/web-UI minor, not an ASAP hotfix. SOAK COMPLETE (2026-06-11): first full-suite run after the harness ruler fixes (typographic-hyphen folding in anchor checks; MCP-namespaced tool recognition — both proven by replaying recorded failing turns) is 38/38, all 23 families grade A, 0 release-gate findings. OPEN OPERATOR DECISION: the production 'unresolved: hello' working-memory junk (1 unresolved_question + 131 derivative rows driving a 60s agency loop) has NO product repair surface — self-healing --repair does not cover working_memory classes; cleanup requires an explicitly authorized surgical DB repair (backup prepared) or waits for the agent-self-repair roadmap feature, which now lists this as repair class #3. SCOPE EXPANDED (operator decision 2026-06-11): the v1.1.2 systemic agency fix folded into v1.1.1 — ad-hoc production surgery rejected in favor of a shipped fix that heals the entire user base; the open operator decision on manual cleanup is CLOSED (production self-heals via the same path). FINAL EVIDENCE (2026-06-11): 38/38 all-A on 4eb8da5e — the HEAD carrying H1 (bind fail-loud), H2 (cache removal), H3 (systemic agency fix), harness ruler fixes, and B15 (substance-based echo detection with B4-projection provenance exemption; two more recorded echo variants locked verbatim: title-only and markdown-reference-only). geopolitical_sitrep 6/6 isolated post-B15. SHIPPED (2026-06-11): tag v1.1.1 on 244150d0; release workflow green first run (all 5 platforms, publish, self-evaluation); verified live — 18 assets, checksum spot-verified, binary reports 1.1.1 on go1.26.4, /releases/latest resolves v1.1.1; site release-sync succeeded.

releases.name: v1.1.3 injection-security + update/install + CI reuse
releases.targetWindow: Point release; formal cut after pre-ship soak per docs/testing/release-procedure.md
releases.summary: Security + reliability release centered on the prompt-injection detector, the update/install path, and the CI/release machinery that ships them. SECURITY: FuzzInjectionDetector_Sanitize surfaced an unauthenticated DoS — falsified-not-dismissed (a single input froze Sanitize ~25s); root cause was unbounded NFKC normalization + many regex passes over untrusted input, and Sanitize runs on every inbound message (src/engine/pipeline_run_stages.go), so one oversized message stalled the pipeline. Fixed by capping scanned input at 256KB and treating oversized input as a threat (maximal score / full redaction) before any normalize/regex. Security review of that fix found a second pre-existing bypass: Sanitize did not decode HTML/percent entities before matching (CheckInput did), so entity-encoded injections survived sanitization unredacted and reached the model — fixed by decoding first. UPDATE: compareVersions sorted a prerelease (1.1.2-dev.<sha>) ABOVE its release, so `upgrade all` on a dev build reported up-to-date and never installed the real release (the operator hit exactly this — production stuck on 1.1.2-dev.06be37e8); the comparator is now SemVer-correct so a dev build upgrades. INSTALL: the in-app updater gained --version (install/reinstall/downgrade a specific release) to match install.sh, and install.sh now installs IN PLACE over an existing flowforge on PATH instead of hardcoding /usr/local/bin (which would shadow the self-updated binary). CI/RELEASE: the release preflight reuses CI evidence keyed on the commit git-TREE instead of the commit SHA, so a develop->main promotion merge commit (or a docs-only release artifact) no longer re-runs the full race on code-identical content — this release is the first to exercise it. TEST TRUST: a network-dependent ghola SSRF unit test (unbounded live DNS) was made bounded + injectable + fail-closed, and a class of brittle unit tests that asserted the prose of a docs markdown file was removed (behavior assertions kept; no unit test reads the doc). Every fix proven from recorded state or a reproducer and TDD'd; full module suite green + race detector clean. FORMAL TAG/PUBLISH PENDING the pre-ship behavioral soak. DEFERRED (justified, in roadmap.json): injection-output-cap-fail-loud (oversized model/tool OUTPUT can be scored as blocked and dropped silently — harden the output path separately), and the email inbox-read capability + honest-blocker escape valve (held for v1.1.4: live verification against the Proton bridge + arch docs pending).

releases.name: v1.1.4 email inbox-read + honest degradation
releases.targetWindow: Behavioral point release; formal cut after pre-ship soak per docs/testing/release-procedure.md
releases.summary: Behavioral release that fixes a real production failure from both directions: a turn where the agent, asked to help with a task it lacked the tools for, looped to a canned 'After 6 attempts, we couldn't get an answer we were confident enough to send' instead of being useful. CAPABILITY: FlowForge had email only as a send/receive channel (inbound polling) with no way for the agent to read or summarize a mailbox, so 'summarize my last 10 days of email' was impossible regardless of tool surface. The new read_inbox tool calls a read-only mail.Service.ListRecent (inbound mail SINCE date scope, BODY.PEEK so mail is never marked seen, bounded full bodies), registered only when inbound mail email is configured; LIVE-VERIFIED end to end against a Proton Bridge (the real config+keystore path read 5 real messages with full bodies, read-only, no state.db touch). HONEST DEGRADATION: RCA from production turn 696abbc9 (turn_diagnostics/turn_diagnostic_events) — asked to propose action on an introspection-only turn (only read-only introspection tools, no action tools), the agent narrated future actions it could not execute; the task_deferral guard rejected every attempt and the grounding judge correctly declined to forgive; after 6 attempts the turn exhausted into the canned fallback. That exhaustion now yields an honest limitation answer (the action can be described/proposed but not executed because only read-only introspection tools were available; enable the needed tool/capability) — the answer the task_deferral guard's own grounding judge already accepts as honest. INSTRUMENTATION (RCA Rule 14): the blocked diagnostics name this case correctly (it was mislabeled as an artifact-write block) and primary_diagnosis is now set on every blocked-exhaustion turn (the RCA turn had an empty diagnosis because it is derived only from retry counters and guard_retry_count was 0). The two halves pair: the capability gives the agent the missing tool; the honest-blocker makes it degrade honestly when a tool is missing. Both root-caused from recorded state and TDD'd; full module suite green and the src/engine race detector clean. Both root-caused from recorded state and TDD'd; full module suite green and the src/engine race detector clean. PRE-SHIP SOAK (2026-06-13, clone mode, cloud-provider-v4-flash, production quiesced): full 38-scenario battery 38/38, all 23 behavior families grade A, 0 release-gate findings (3 stress_only log findings: one empty_finalization, two multi_step_model_call_wait — timing, not behavior). SHIPPED (2026-06-13): merged via PR #111 (develop->main, merge commit be1bccbc); tag v1.1.4 on be1bccbc; release workflow green; CI tree-reuse fired (Pre-release Validation reused the green develop push CI for tree f869e4f8 — the heavy validation steps go vet / race tests / smoke / architecture were SKIPPED, second consecutive release validating the tree-reuse machinery); published live — assets incl. SHA256SUMS, the darwin-arm64 binary reports 1.1.4 on go1.26.4 (clean, no -dev), /releases/latest resolves FlowForge v1.1.4 (prerelease=false); site release-sync dispatch succeeded (pipeline-owned deploy). DEFERRED (justified, in roadmap.json): injection-output-cap-fail-loud (oversized model/tool OUTPUT can be scored as blocked and dropped silently) and guard-retry-count-accuracy-for-judge-mediated-retries (the counter miscount whose downstream empty-diagnosis symptom is already fixed).

releases.name: v1.1.5 injection-defense completeness + flaky-test elimination
releases.targetWindow: Patch release; formal cut after pre-ship behavioral soak per docs/testing/release-procedure.md
releases.summary: Security + reliability PATCH on the behavioral-correctness line. SECURITY: closes two prompt-injection-defense deferrals. (1) injection-output-cap-fail-loud (#113): the v1.1.3 input DoS cap (256KB) also governed the L4 output scan, so a legitimately large clean tool/LLM OUTPUT was scored as a maximal threat and either mislabeled '[Tool output filtered: potential injection detected]' or silently emptied; output now has a separate 1MiB cap and a bounded-prefix scan (clean large output passes, prefix injection still caught, oversized emits an honest tool_output_scan_truncated event), input stays fail-closed (deliberately asymmetric). (2) injection-positional-override-detection (#115): CheckInput matched 'ignore...previous' but not positional overrides ('ignore everything above', entity-encoded included), which scored 0 and reached the model unsanitized; a positional pattern now scores them caution so they are redacted (found by the adversarial exercise, root-caused deterministically). RELIABILITY: the flaky-test problem is eliminated wholesale and gated — an audit of all 64 test-sleep sites converted the flaky ones across nine packages to assert.Eventually polling (+ atomic watched-file writes, a load-invariant concurrency ratio, explicit eviction timestamps, a size-detectable keystore change), and a CI guard (scripts/check-test-sleeps.sh) blocks new time.Sleep-then-assert beyond the reviewed baseline (#116-#119). COVERAGE/PROCESS: adversarial-issue coverage to 0 gaps / 100% (#114), and the product-knowledge pack audit made a mandatory release-checklist item (#110). No new user-facing feature surface (that is v1.2.0, the packaged embedder). PRE-SHIP SOAK (2026-06-14, clone, cloud-provider-v4-flash, production quiesced): full 38-scenario battery 37/38, release_gate_findings=0, 22/23 families grade A; the one non-A (continuity_topic_anchor) is a documented verifier-sensitivity re-verified PASS 3/3 in isolation (variance, unrelated to the injection-path changes). SHIPPED (2026-06-14): promoted via PR #121 (develop->main, merge 66257224), tag v1.1.5; release workflow green, CI tree-reuse fired (Pre-release Validation reused the green develop push CI for tree 6798d9da — heavy validation steps skipped, third consecutive release validating the machinery); published live — the darwin-arm64 binary reports 1.1.5 clean on go1.26.4 (no -dev), SHA256SUMS.txt present, /releases/latest resolves v1.1.5 (prerelease=false), site release-sync dispatch succeeded (pipeline-owned deploy).

releases.name: v1.1.6 voice-runtime upgrade hotfix
releases.summary: Patch hotfix for a single upgrade-path defect: voice runtime silently failed to refresh on upgrade/update all with 'Warning: ... unsafe archive path "./"' — latent on every upgrade since v1.1.0, surfaced by an operator on the 1.1.4->1.1.5 upgrade. The voice-piper archive carries a './' root entry (the conventional first tar record); extractTarGz pre-skipped it but the two voice extractors (extractVoiceRuntimeTarGz, extractVoiceRuntimeZip) passed it into the shared safeArchiveTarget, which rejected '.' as unsafe — one extract loop diverged. SYSTEMIC FIX: the archive-root rule now lives once in safeArchiveTarget (reports the harmless root entry as skip; still rejects absolute/'..'/prefix-escape); all three extract loops consume the single rule and extractTarGz's divergent special-case is removed, so the inconsistency that caused the bug cannot recur. TDD RED (TestExtractVoiceRuntimeTarGz_RootEntry failed with the exact production error) -> GREEN; traversal-rejection tests unchanged; cmd/updatecmd green under -race; build green. No agent-runtime surface touched (cmd/updatecmd CLI only), so no behavioral soak is applicable (justified). The self-update domain unification into a testable src/update.Updater (merging the extract loops) remains tracked for v1.2.0. SHIPPED (2026-06-14): promoted via PR #124 (develop->main, merge f7bdc362), tag v1.1.6; release workflow green, CI tree-reuse fired (4th consecutive release; Pre-release Validation reused develop CI for tree 3a176c26); published live — clean 1.1.6 binary, SHA256SUMS present, /releases/latest resolves v1.1.6 (prerelease=false), site release-sync dispatched. END-TO-END proof: the published voice-piper bundle's first tar entry is './' (the exact root that failed) and the fixed code extracts it (TDD RED->GREEN on that exact error). No behavioral soak applicable (CLI upgrade path only; agent runtime byte-identical to v1.1.5).

releases.name: v1.2.0 bundled embedder sidecar + audit remediation
releases.targetWindow: Shipping 2026-06-17. All release-gate blockers complete; full behavioral soak suite GREEN on the tagged commit 45b16afd (battery 38/38 incl. release_gate 31/31, adversarial 38/38 / 0 gate-failures, memory-grounding 6/6, prompt-compression 3/3+3/3, embedder 4/4, fuzz 0-crash). The two live-verification items (metered-billing on-chain clear, Landlock Linux runtime) are operator-accepted POST-SHIP live gates — their hard ship-blocking risk is retired in code; residuals need real funds / a Linux host and run out-of-band.
releases.summary: Headline: bundle a embed-model embedder as a flowforge-owned, daemon-supervised llama.cpp sidecar (removes the Embedded Runtime embedding dependency; GGUF content-hash-pinned; the platform binary stays CGO_ENABLED=0). Operator-confirmed scope (2026-06-14) folds in: the cycle-opening triple-lens audit remediation batch, the 3 CRITICALs (proven RED->GREEN, no separate hotfix), the financial-scaffolding wiring (TreasuryPolicy + EVMSubmitter with float->Money + revert-status), and the contradiction-detection cosine-threshold recalibration inside the embedder's workstream-4 sweep. In-cycle additions: an install/upgrade CLI-design-language facelift, a logging-centralization + controlled-output sweep (daemon log-vomit fix, daemon-control before/after status output), and the go-facade-template logging adoption (context-propagated correlation IDs + console/JSON format selection + ProblemError). (Mid-cycle at time of writing — all now RESOLVED: the two live-verification items, the remaining sidecar workstreams, the financial wiring, and the architecture remediation.) | SESSION 2026-06-15: shipped an RCA-driven behavioral-correctness + recall-grounding batch (transport-leak, derivable-tools, porter-stemming FTS migration 069, UTF-8/abbreviation truncation, cron force-delivery) all RED->GREEN; fixed the stale dev embedder config (bundled is the standing default). NEWLY IN SCOPE (operator, correctness-trumps-all): the framework-reliability cluster A-D (routing-to-unfunded, framework-built invalid requests, 4xx misclassification, canned-gate residual) + a layman upgrade prompt. A mid-cycle blocker required re-running the full soak suite on the bundled sidecar (the 2026-06-15 soaks had used the legacy Embedded Runtime embedder) — done. SHIPPED 2026-06-17: all blockers complete; full behavioral soak suite green on the tagged commit 45b16afd (battery 38/38, adversarial 38/38 / 0 gate-failures, memory-grounding 6/6, prompt-compression 3/3+3/3, embedder 4/4, fuzz 0-crash); metered-billing on-chain clear + Landlock Linux runtime are operator-accepted post-ship live gates (hard risk retired in code).

releases.name: v1.1.2 reliability + channels
releases.targetWindow: Formal cut after audit of PR #94 per docs/testing/release-procedure.md
releases.summary: Reliability hotfix + agency confinement + DB writer/read pool split + email STARTTLS. Every fix was proven from recorded state (production daemon log + state.db), TDD'd (RED->GREEN), with the full module suite green (60 ok / 0 fail) and the db race detector clean. BEHAVIORAL CORRECTNESS: the inbound dedup claim now fails open (a transient store_snapshot on the idempotency INSERT was silently dropping user messages — two Chat Connector messages eaten during a maintenance lock storm, RCA 2026-06-11); read-only agency action classes (observe/summarize/diagnose) are confined to read-only tools at execution (the action-class policy gated the decision but not the tool surface, so a 'summarize' approval spawned three self-funding workflows via orchestrate-subagents/retry-task on 2026-06-09). SCHEDULER: the cron worker dropped every cron-expression job for ~2 months because a non-nullable scan of NULL schedule_every_ms silently failed; fixed with a nullable scan + fail-loud, and legacy cron_runs columns are converged (error->error_msg, created_at->timestamp, output_text canonicalized) so the revived worker records run history. DB CONTENTION (corruption-window fix): state.db has corrupted in production; the single shared pool is split into a dedicated 1-connection serialized writer + a concurrent read pool, the O(n^2) correlated hygiene dedup that held the WAL writer ~90s is rewritten set-based, the backup VACUUM INTO moved off the writer, and store_snapshot is surfaced honestly. CHANNELS: STARTTLS is a real mail security mode end to end (inbound mail + outbound mail, configurable outbound mail security), enabling a local Proton Bridge. LIVE VALIDATION (operator instance, binary 1.1.2-dev.06be37e8): clean startup on the 468MB production DB, cron_runs converged in place, the scheduler revived (workspace cleanup ran for the first time since 2026-04-06 and recorded to the converged schema), agency confinement active, and the email channel validated both directions over STARTTLS (inbound mail auth + allowed_senders filtering, outbound mail send). PRE-SHIP SOAK (2026-06-11, cloud route, production quiesced): the v1.1.2 cron guard-exhaustion regression is FIXED — 0 release-gate log findings (the first soak had 9; the autonomous capability-denial exemption eliminated them), 22/23 families grade A (capability_denial_specificity and execution_truth stay A, so user-facing false-denial detection is intact). The single rotating non-A is current_events_truth (fresh_external_info), grounded as a soak-isolation artifact: the isolated instance sandbox-killed the external ghola fetch at 20s ('signal: killed') while host ghola AND curl fetch the same Open-Meteo URL in under 1s — not a network, tool, model, or v1.1.2-code fault. continuity_topic_anchor is a verifier-sensitivity on a reply-only literal that passes on re-run. Full module suite green (60 ok / 0 fail) + db race detector clean. The two residual rotating failures do not trace to any v1.1.2 change (the changes touch autonomous turns, DB plumbing, inbound handling, and email — not the user-facing turn path these scenarios exercise). FORMAL TAG/PUBLISH PENDING. DEFERRED (justified, tracked in roadmap.json): agency blocked-proposal notification (its recorded trigger is mooted by the cron revival), cron-job creation validation (the briefings still carry delivery_mode='none'), and 'service stop' respawn under launchd KeepAlive (service-management behavior with its own test surface).

releases.name: v1.3.0 local LLM hosting + Models page, compaction engine, audit + soak infra
releases.targetWindow: Planning crystallized 2026-06-17 from the approved brainstorm sketch; work not yet started. Audit-first, dependency-ordered (Phase 0 open -> Phase 3 validate/close). No firm target date set.
releases.summary: Feature-tier release (minor, not a patch): promote the v1.2.0 bundled llama.cpp sidecar from embeddings-only to a first-class local LLM host, and stand up a dedicated Models page (model config decomposed out of monolithic Settings, local-host management UI, ModelHub discover/compat/download/SHA-pin/install/activate flow, BYO-GGUF-URL path) that manages embedded AND external local backends as first-class peers (additive: external llama.cpp/Embedded Runtime support is NOT removed). Alongside: an intelligent parallel compaction engine (wheat/chaff off the critical path -> persistence + runtime context reduction), the long-planned execution-grounded real coding benchmark, a post-v1.2.0 wholesale benchmark re-run (now also exercising the local-host route), per-use-case model recommendations turning benchmark scores into a decision (up to 3 local + 3 cloud for coding/general-assistant/chatbot, deterministic over quant/qual/latency, additive and routing-neutral), a fresh cycle-opening triple-lens code audit, soak infrastructure (orchestration harness + gate-on-resolution + the answer-fidelity correct-answer-survival inverse-soak), two contained correctness fixes (per-turn retry-cost ceiling, verifier evidence recovery on TOTOF-collapse), and a full-scale product-knowledge pack refresh (all versions, source-backed, current). Also folds in the SelfHealing: a framework-native, deterministic maintenance/repair subsystem (modeled on RunHygiene, no LLM/no authority negotiation, idempotent) that supersedes the subagent-delegation framing and generalizes self-heal across DATABASE (5 hygiene coverage gaps + 3 recurring sources, with source fixes), WORKSPACE, CONFIGURATION, PLUGINS, SKILLS, and SECURITY (sensitive domains report-and-propose only); per the v1.3.0+ charter the tail domains may extend past v1.3.0.
